notes: These evals depend on live ADO state. The key thing being tested is classification accuracy, correct delegation routing, state persistence, worktree management, and dependency ordering.

evals.name: mixed-sprint-all-stages
evals.expected_output: Classifies work items into 3 stages. Processes Stage 3 first (babysit-pr-worker single pass, up to 3 parallel), then Stage 2 (work-on, sequential), then Stage 1 (work-on planning, parallel). Saves state.json after completion.

assertions.name: stage3_processed_first
assertions.description: Stage 3 items are processed before Stage 2 and Stage 1

assertions.name: state_file_written
assertions.description: scratchpad/work-my-backlog/state.json exists and contains valid JSON with work_items, last_run, pass_count

assertions.name: correct_delegation
assertions.description: Stage 3 uses babysit-pr-worker agent, Stage 2 and 1 use work-on skill

assertions.name: summary_presented
assertions.description: Final summary shows per-item results grouped by stage

evals.name: second-pass-state-continuity
evals.precondition: state.json already exists from a previous pass with pass_count=1, work items in various stages
evals.expected_output: Reads existing state.json, cross-checks with live ADO data, detects stage changes (e.g., item moved from Stage 2 to Stage 3 because PR was published), updates state, increments pass_count to 2.

assertions.name: state_loaded
assertions.description: Reads existing state.json before querying ADO

assertions.name: pass_count_incremented
assertions.description: pass_count in saved state is previous + 1

assertions.name: stage_transition_detected
assertions.description: Work item that gained a PR since last pass is reclassified from Stage 2 to Stage 3

assertions.name: addressed_threads_preserved
assertions.description: Previously addressed thread IDs from state are passed to babysit-pr-worker

evals.name: worktree-per-workitem
evals.precondition: 2 Stage 2 items with approved plans (ready to implement), no existing worktrees
evals.expected_output: Creates separate worktrees for each work item (.worktrees/wi-<id>/), records paths in state.json, passes worktree paths to work-on agents. Each agent works in its own isolated directory.

assertions.name: worktree_created_per_item
assertions.description: Each Stage 2 item gets its own worktree at .worktrees/wi-<id>/

assertions.name: worktree_recorded_in_state
assertions.description: state.json contains worktree_path and branch for each implementing item

assertions.name: worktree_reused_on_second_pass
assertions.description: On next pass, existing worktrees are reused, not recreated

evals.name: dependency-ordering
evals.precondition: 3 work items: Parent user story #100, child task #101 (predecessor of #102), child task #102. All in Stage 1.
evals.expected_output: #101 is planned before #102 (predecessor relationship). Both children planned before parent #100 can advance. Dependency notes appear in summary.

assertions.name: predecessor_first
assertions.description: #101 is processed before #102 within Stage 1

assertions.name: children_before_parent
assertions.description: Child tasks are planned before parent user story

assertions.name: dependency_notes_in_summary
assertions.description: Summary mentions dependency relationships and any blocking items

evals.name: stale-sprint-fallback
evals.prompt: work on everything assigned to me
evals.precondition: Current sprint is past its end date (e.g., Monthly ended Dec 2025), but user has New/Active items
evals.expected_output: Detects stale sprint, falls back to state-based query (New/Active), finds and classifies items correctly.

assertions.name: fallback_query_used
assertions.description: When sprint-based query returns nothing or sprint is expired, falls back to state-based query

assertions.name: items_found
assertions.description: Work items with New/Active state are found despite stale sprint

evals.name: auto-mode-no-confirmation
evals.expected_output: Proceeds immediately after classification without asking for user confirmation. Processes all stages autonomously.

assertions.name: no_confirmation_prompt
assertions.description: Skill does NOT ask 'should I proceed?' or wait for user input before processing

assertions.name: autonomous_processing
assertions.description: All stages are processed without pausing for user interaction

evals.name: completed-items-cleaned-up
evals.precondition: state.json has an item whose PR was merged since last pass. Item is now Resolved in ADO.
evals.expected_output: Detects merged PR, removes worktree, removes item from state, reports completion in summary.

assertions.name: merged_pr_detected
assertions.description: Work item with merged PR is identified as done

assertions.name: worktree_cleaned
assertions.description: git worktree remove is called for completed item's worktree

assertions.name: removed_from_state
assertions.description: Completed work item is removed from state.work_items

evals.name: error-resilience
evals.precondition: 3 work items, one of which consistently fails (e.g., ADO API error on fetch)
evals.expected_output: Failing item is skipped with error logged, error_count incremented in state. Other items processed normally. After 3 consecutive errors across passes, item is flagged as stuck.

assertions.name: error_does_not_block_others
assertions.description: Other work items are processed despite one item failing

assertions.name: error_count_tracked
assertions.description: error_count in state is incremented for the failing item
